query_normalized: the influence of visual salience on attentional bias in moral decision-making psychology
rationale: This paper is off-domain entirely, focusing on AI ethics and diagnostic challenges in gastroenterology rather than human cognitive processes like visual salience or attentional bias in moral decision-making. The shared terms "bias" and "decision-making" refer to distinct constructs (algorithmic fairness vs. cognitive psychology), satisfying the rejection rule for distinct constructs sharing only homonym keywords.
